channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Mbekweni murder suspect declines Legal Aid and remains in custody
item.description: Mnikelo Khese, the man accused of murdering Ntombi Ntsondwa, the mother of his five-year-old child, made his first appearance at Paarl Magistrate’s Court on Wednesday. The proceedings were delayed aft...

item.title: Nandyal MP Byreddy Shabari appointed as TDP's first female national general secretary
item.description: In a historic development for the Telugu Desam Party (TDP), Nandyal MP Byreddy Shabari has been appointed as the party's national general secretary, marking the first time a woman has held this positi...

item.title: Maendeleo Bank plans tier 1 status by 2030 after strong asset growth
item.description: Maendeleo Bank Ltd, based in Dar es Salaam, is aiming to achieve Tier 1 status by 2030 following significant growth in its assets last year. The bank's total assets increased by 34 percent to TZS 202....

item.title: Kenya Faces Sh3.2bn Loss After Government Cancels Emergency Fuel Deal
item.description: Nairobi — A big kerfuffle has hit Kenya's fuel sector after Oryx Energies Kenya Ltd revealed that the government abruptly cancelled a multi-million shilling emergency fuel contract while shipments wer...

item.title: Six South African swimmers qualify for Commonwealth Games after national championships
item.description: At the Swimming South Africa national championships in Gqeberha, Aimee Canny continued her stellar performances by winning the women's 200m individual medley, narrowly defeating defending champion Reb...

item.title: Macron and European allies coordinate on post-war Strait of Hormuz strategy, excluding Trump
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron announced that France and the United Kingdom will host a diplomatic meeting in Paris this Friday to address the ongoing crisis in the Strait of Hormuz, deliberately ex...

item.title: WA Government names first state priority projects under new development laws, including four wind farms and green iron pilot
item.description: The Western Australian Premier Roger Cook has announced the first five projects to receive special priority status under the state's new State Development Act. The legislation, passed in late 2025, gi...

item.title: Residents Evacuated as Haze from Navotas Landfill Spreads to Bulacan Coastal Areas
item.description: Smoke from a burning sanitary landfill in Navotas City has caused haze in several coastal villages in Obando, Bulacan, prompting authorities to evacuate some residents. According to the Municipal Disa...

item.title: Zanzibar Confirmed as Host for World Travel Awards Africa 2026
item.description: Zanzibar has reached a major milestone in its tourism sector after being confirmed as the host for the World Travel Awards Africa 2026, scheduled for August 28, 2026. This marks the first time Zanziba...

item.title: Kerala MP John Brittas criticises Modi government's plan to increase North Indian representation in Parliament
item.description: The Modi government has proposed a significant change in the allocation of parliamentary seats across India, aiming to increase representation for northern states. This proposal seeks to end the freez...

item.title: House Justice Panel to Review VP Sara Duterte’s Wealth Using Bank Records
item.description: The House justice panel is set to review allegations of unexplained wealth against Vice President Sara Duterte, focusing on her and her husband, Mans Carpio's, bank transactions from 2006 to 2025. The...

item.title: Manachanallur Constituency grapples with water and infrastructure concerns amid urbanisation
item.description: The Manachanallur Assembly Constituency in Tiruchi district of Tamil Nadu, created in 2008 after a delimitation exercise, is facing increasing challenges due to rapid urbanisation and shifting priorit...

item.title: CBK Approves 32 Additional Digital Lenders, Total Reaches 227 in Kenya
item.description: The Central Bank of Kenya (CBK) has approved another batch of 32 Digital Credit Providers (DCPs), taking the total number of licensed digital lenders in the country to 227. This move follows a previou...

item.title: Kasganj: 23 Arrested Following Stone-Pelting at Ambedkar Jayanti Procession
item.description: A rather unpleasant incident unfolded in Kasganj, Uttar Pradesh, during the Ambedkar Jayanti celebrations. According to reports, a procession commemorating Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's birth anniversary came u...

item.title: African Union Ministers to Convene in South Africa on Transport and Energy Infrastructure
item.description: From 27 to 30 April 2026, Johannesburg will host the Fifth Session of the African Union's Specialised Technical Committee on Transport and Energy, marking a significant step for Africa's infrastructur...

item.title: Taifa Gas acquires stake in Songo Songo gas project after Orca exit
item.description: Tanzania's gas sector is seeing more local participation after Orca Energy Group Inc decided to exit its Songo Songo gas project operations. Orca sold all shares in its Mauritian subsidiary, PanAfrica...

item.title: Kargil Democratic Alliance Reaffirms Demand for Ladakh Statehood and Sixth Schedule Protections
item.description: The Kargil Democratic Alliance (KDA), a coalition of political and religious groups in Kargil, has firmly rejected the Centre's proposal for a territorial or regional council in Ladakh. During a recen...

item.title: Two Metropolitan Police Officers Charged After Fatal Crash Involving Pregnant Woman
item.description: Two officers from the Metropolitan Police have been formally charged following a tragic collision in south-east London that claimed the lives of a heavily pregnant woman and her unborn child. PC Chris...

item.title: GP removed from medical register after misconduct involving Muslim patient and working while suspended
item.description: Dr Keith Wolverson, a GP, has been struck off the medical register after a tribunal found he continued working during a suspension imposed for professional misconduct. The misconduct involved asking a...

item.title: Ensuring Patient Rights Amid India's Clinical Trial Expansion
item.description: India is seeking to significantly expand its clinical trial infrastructure, with the 2026-'27 budget allocating Rs 10,000 crore over five years to establish 1,000 new accredited trial sites and streng...
